commits: 2c3cdbfa02cbb6a88e8532aaf40b510f8506e509: test(0.8.0): Slice 10 fix-1 RED — FFI SearchFilter string validation (AC-068a/b)
2715bbdb84bebce37e49a82d164a5a2b01a6d417: fix(0.8.0): Slice 10 fix-1 GREEN — validate FFI SearchFilter strings (codex P2 x2)

findings_addressed.summary: Python: G10 SearchFilter string fields (source_type, kind, status) crossed the FFI unvalidated while query + write fields are gated by validate_ffi_string_py (AC-068a NUL / AC-068b lone UTF-16 surrogate).
findings_addressed.fix: Filter string params changed from Option<String> to Option<Bound<PyAny>> and routed through extract_validated_str (the same write-path helper), via a new Option-lifting helper extract_opt_validated_str. Both NUL and lone surrogate now raise the typed WriteValidationError. created_after (i64) unchanged. All-None filter stays None -> byte-identical unfiltered path preserved.

findings_addressed.summary: TypeScript/napi: G10 SearchFilter string fields crossed the FFI unvalidated while query + write fields are gated by validate_ffi_string_napi.
findings_addressed.fix: napi search() validates each Some filter string via validate_ffi_string_napi before the all-None collapse (NUL defense-in-depth; collapse + byte-identical path intact). Because napi-rs lossily replaces lone UTF-16 surrogates with U+FFFD before the Rust guard runs, the surrogate check (AC-068b) is enforced JS-side in the TS search() wrapper via validateFfiString — mirroring the existing write/configure/query pattern documented in src/ts/src/validation.ts.

tdd_evidence.red_observed: Python: 6 failed, 4 deselected (NUL unguarded; surrogate raised raw UnicodeEncodeError instead of WriteValidationError). TS: 6 new filter cases failed (49 pass / 6 fail) on the unvalidated napi+wrapper code. After GREEN: Python 10/10 in test_ffi_safety.py (full suite 84 passed, 5 skipped); TS 55/55.

blockers_encountered.id: DEV-1 (justified deviation, escalate-class: shared SDK surface)
blockers_encountered.description: The prompt scoped edits to the two binding lib.rs files + two test files. But AC-068b (lone UTF-16 surrogate) cannot be satisfied for TS filter strings at the napi/Rust layer: napi-rs lossily replaces lone surrogates with U+FFFD before the Rust validate_ffi_string guard sees them (this is documented in src/ts/src/validation.ts, and is exactly why the existing write/configure/query paths guard surrogates JS-side). The TS RED test for the surrogate cases stayed failing after the Rust-only napi fix.
blockers_encountered.resolution: RESOLVED — added the minimal JS-side guard in the TS search() wrapper (src/ts/src/index.ts): validateFfiString on filter.sourceType/kind/status before the native call, mirroring the established write/configure/query pattern. Kept the napi Rust guard as NUL defense-in-depth (matches query + write). This touches src/ts/src/index.ts, outside the prompt's named file set. Flagged loud here per justified-deviation protocol; it is the smallest change that makes the cited AC-068b contract hold for TS, and it does not change the public SDK signature (only adds validation on an existing path). Python needed no SDK-layer change because PyO3 raises UnicodeEncodeError on surrogate extraction, which extract_validated_str re-maps to WriteValidationError.

blockers_encountered.description: Two pre-existing untracked orchestrator files are present under dev/plans/ in the canonical repo (the fix-1 prompt and the codex review .md/.log). They are not src/ edits and were not created by me.
blockers_encountered.resolution: No action — they are the orchestrator's handoff artifacts. Canonical src/ stayed clean throughout (stray-edit tripwire empty on every check). An incidental package-lock.json version bump (0.6.1->0.7.2) produced by `npm install` was reverted to keep scope to exactly the 5 intended files.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: src/ts/src/index.ts search() wrapper: added JS-side validateFfiString on filter.sourceType/kind/status (justified deviation DEV-1; required for AC-068b on TS — napi-rs lossy-converts lone surrogates before the Rust guard).

fathomdb-py: added Option-lifting helper extract_opt_validated_str next to extract_validated_str; search() filter params changed Option<String> -> Option<Bound<PyAny>> so surrogate extraction errors become the typed WriteValidationError (parity with the write path).
napi loop idiom adjusted to .into_iter().flatten() to satisfy clippy::manual_flatten under -D warnings; comment corrected to state surrogate is guarded JS-side.
agent_verify_result: pass. cargo test -p fathomdb-engine -p fathomdb-schema: all green (incl. pr_g10_filtered_knn 6/6, byte-identity pin unfiltered_phase1_sql_byte_identical_to_0_7_2 passing). pytest full Python suite: 84 passed, 5 skipped (test_ffi_safety.py 10/10, incl. 6 new filter cases). TS npm test: 55 passed, 0 fail (incl. 6 new filter cases). cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean. cargo fmt --check: clean. mkdocs build --strict: clean. ruff check src/python: All checks passed. filter=None byte-identity preserved.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Slice 10 fix-1 merged to local main at 46ce583. Re-run codex on the fix-1 diff (note the DEV-1 justified deviation: the napi P2 fix required a JS-side guard in src/ts/src/index.ts because napi-rs lossy-converts lone surrogates — confirm that is acceptable, it mirrors the existing write/configure/query pattern). Then 10.b / close Slice 10 and advance the pointer. Worktree /tmp/fdb-slice-10-fix1-20260603T123527Z left in place for orchestrator removal at close.
